title: helleborus orientalis subsp. orientalis
intro: Helleborus orientalis subsp. orientalis, commonly known as the Lenten rose, is a flowering plant species within the Helleborus orientalis complex. It is a perennial evergreen with nodding, bell-shaped flowers that bloom in late winter to early spring, typically in shades of white or pale pink. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain. Helleborus orientalis subsp. orientalis can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
